arts.ttl: Serie A: Roma vs Udinese – probable line-ups and where to watch on TV
arts.sum: Matias Soulé is expected to start on the bench again in Ivan Juric’s first game...

Roma have won 18 of their last 22 games against Udinese in Serie A (D1 L3) – since the 2013/14 season, the Giallorossi have won more games (18) and kept more clean sheets (11) against this opponent than any other. Roma have scored 182 goals in their 100 previous meetings with Udinese in Serie A: an average of 1.82 goals per game - the highest for the Giallorossi against any opponent they’ve faced more than 60 times in the top-flight.

arts.ttl: Roma CEO Under Police Protection Amid Fan Backlash
arts.sum: These are delicate times for Roma CEO Lina Souloukou after provoking fans' anger with the recent dismissal of Daniele De Rossi. According to La Repubblica, fears over Souloukou's safety have convinced local authorities to place her under police protection. arts.ttl: Roma CEO Lina Souloukou under police protection amid dan backlash
arts.sum: Roma’s CEO Lina Souloukou has been placed under police protection following threats and insults from a section of the club’s supporters. This development comes just days after the controversial dismissal of head coach Daniele De Rossi, a decision that has sparked outrage among some fans. Sportface report that while a full security detail has not yet been assigned, authorities are closely monitoring the situation. The Committee for Order and Security at the prefecture will conduct a thorough assessment in the coming days to determine if a permanent escort is necessary. This decision will be based on detailed discussions and evaluations The post Roma CEO Lina Souloukou under police protection amid dan backlash appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Udinese’s Runjaic: “I want a happy and optimistic team”
arts.sum: Udinese manager Kosta Runjaic previewed his team’s upcoming match against Roma, emphasizing focus on their own performance rather than their opponents’ situation. Despite Udinese’s strong start to the season, Runjaic remains grounded, stating, “We’re happy to be top and to give our fans a great experience, but we’re not looking at the league table, nor our opponents’ situation. We’re focused on the match.” The coach acknowledged the challenge of facing Roma, especially with their new manager, but expressed confidence in his team’s readiness. “We want to be brave and deliver a good performance.
